description: §5.4.1 / §6.2 E-CELL-RENDER-SPEC-NOT-BINDABLE via the compound wrapping form. A compound child with a non-bindable render-spec (`<name req> = <div/>`) used as `<signup><name/></>` (SPEC §6.4 wrapping form) is rejected — the same as a top-level `<name/>` use. Previously the compound-qualified child slipped the file-scope-only render-by-tag check and emitted garbage `<name/>` HTML.
rationale: §6.2 Shape 2 requires a bindable form element as the render-spec root; `<div>` is not bindable. The render-by-tag bindability check must resolve the compound child `signup.name` through the enclosing compound wrapper's sub-scope, not just file scope.
